key_risk_factors: Potential cross‑border data transfers with no region‑specific residency (Privacy & Data Governance)
Customer data may be used for model improvement unless a manual opt‑out is submitted (Privacy & Data Governance)
Only SOC 2 Type I certification; no Type II, ISO 27001, or GDPR attestations (Compliance & Certification)
Limited operational track record (18 months, 12 enterprise customers) and undocumented API rate limits (Operational & Technical Risk)
Usage‑based pricing with no caps could lead to unexpected cost spikes (Pricing & Commercial Risk)
Early‑stage vendor with limited financial and organizational maturity (Vendor Maturity & Stability)
missing_information: Exact timeline and scope for region‑specific data residency (e.g., EU, US, APAC)
Formal data‑processing agreement detailing data ownership, retention, and deletion procedures
Automated mechanism for opting out of data‑for‑training; how opt‑out is enforced and audited
Encryption standards used at rest and in transit (algorithms, key management, HSM usage)
Roadmap to achieve SOC 2 Type II and any other relevant certifications (ISO 27001, GDPR, CCPA)
Incident response plan, breach notification timelines, and liability clauses
Detailed SLA terms: penalties for downtime, MTTR targets, and service credits
API rate‑limit specifications and any over‑age throttling policies
Pricing model details: unit cost, volume discounts, caps, and forecasted cost at projected document volumes
Financial health indicators: funding rounds, revenue, burn rate, and any parent company backing
Exit strategy and data migration/hand‑off procedures at contract termination
parsed_output.business_recommendation: Proceed with a controlled pilot only after obtaining clarifications on data residency, opt‑out automation, and security certifications. Negotiate contractual addenda to secure data‑processing agreements, enforce strict data‑use limitations, and establish cost‑cap provisions. Consider a fallback provider for critical invoice processing to mitigate operational risk. If the vendor cannot meet these controls within a reasonable timeframe, explore alternative vendors with proven compliance and maturity.
